configs.description: Base model, unidirectional NLI, no margin, no SparseCL E-Verify, no predicate gate
Base model, bidirectional NLI, no margin, no SparseCL E-Verify, no predicate gate
Base model, bidirectional + margin 0.40 (symmetric), no E-Verify, no predicate gate
Base model, bidirectional + margin + soft predicate gate (0.15), no E-Verify
Base model: bidirectional + margin + soft predicate + SparseCL rerank (E-Verify off, Apr 2026 preset)
WANLI-tuned DeBERTa-v3-large + same stack as E (rerank, no E-Verify)
WANLI model + asymmetric reverse margin 0.25 when forward NLI is strong (forward margin stays 0.40)

A_base_unidirectional.proposal_body: Entity YAML follows Kubernetes-style envelopes: apiVersion, kind, metadata, and spec sections.
TechDocs production docs are built in CI/CD and stored in external object storage (S3/GCS), not generated on every request in the app container.
TechDocs static sites for production should be published from the pipeline to external buckets rather than baking generation into the Backstage pod.
Software Templates use Cookiecutter and Nunjucks for generating repository scaffolding.
API Docs can render OpenAPI v2/v3, AsyncAPI, and GraphQL specifications.
Catalog entity validation uses JSON Schema (draft-07) evaluated at ingestion time.
IdentityApi remains the supported way to read the current user's identity reference in frontend code.
GitLab Cells uses flexible reference architectures with sub-archetypes and overlays instead of one rigid cell blueprint.
Org Mover supports migrating organizations into Cells without requiring global downtime.
catalogprocessingqueuedelayseconds reflects time from scheduling to processor execution for catalog work.
Software components are registered by POSTing a catalog location URL that points at a catalog-info.yaml.
GET /create/actions lists installed Scaffolder actions with schemas and examples (e.g. publish:github).
Runtime metrics are exposed at /metrics via express-prom-bundle for Prometheus scraping.
Catalog reads are governed by RBAC resources like catalog.entity.read through the permissionsRegistry.
AnalyticsApi forwards usage events to analytics backends including GA4.
Backend HTTP calls use FetchApi so Backstage identity tokens are attached automatically.
Scaffolder task logs can be fetched per task with cursor-based continuation.
Engineers can list active batched background migrations via the GitLab ChatOps command for batched_background_migrations list.
The Backstage backend runs on Node.js with Express-based HTTP services.
Redis (with Sentinel where required) backs Sidekiq job queues in GitLab-scale deployments.
Scaffolder dry-run mode lets authors validate templates without pushing commits to remotes.
Migration CI jobs flag unexpected database growth as a signal of migration risk.
